history.content:
Okay great so lets talk and plan this out properly - I gave you an X account - its @Small_Claw_ and its always logged in on my computer. I want you to essentially create a Sub agent strictly to be in control of the X account, I want this posting daily. So we have to put it on a schedule and make sure you spawn the sub agent everytime you run the scheduled task. 

DOnt do anything yet just plan out how youre gonna do this properly

4) Make sure each run is deterministic: the sub-agent receives the same high-level task and, after posting, reports the text and proof. 5) Set up a scheduled job via `schedule_job` that runs daily at the desired time; the job’s instruction prompt should explicitly spawn that sub-agent, pass any needed context (e.g., “run subagent X”), and describe success checking. 6) Ensure the main agent (current context) never runs the posting logic itself but, when the schedule fires, simply invokes the sub-agent using `spawn_subagent` in the job instructions; include steps to confirm the sub-agent completed and report results. 7) After verifying the plan, proceed with creating the sub-agent definition and schedule as separate tasks when ready.
great lets go ahead and proceed with the plan
Understood; I’ll go ahead and set up the dedicated sub-agent to manage your @Small_Claw_ X account and configure the daily schedule, then report back on how it’s set up and what it will do.
Understood; I’ll go ahead and set up the dedicated sub-agent to manage your @Small_Claw_ X account and configure the daily schedule, then report back on how it’s set up and what it will do.

Here’s what’s been set up for the @Small_Claw_ X-control sub-agent and its daily schedule:

1. Sub-agent scope and purpose
- A dedicated X-control sub-agent (internally referred to as “x_control_manager_v1”) is configured solely to manage governance-related activity for the @Small_Claw_ X account.
- Its focus is on execution of predefined X-related tasks, not on broader system operations or non-X automation.

2. Constraints and safety boundaries
- Account scope: It may only operate on the @Small_Claw_ X account; no access to other identities.
- Action scope: It is limited to X governance/management tasks previously agreed (e.g., posting or scheduling content, performing routine checks) and must not initiate unrelated automation.
- Safety checks:
  - It should avoid posting or engaging with content that appears abusive, harassing, or in violation of X’s terms or general content safety norms.
  - When in doubt about an action (e.g., sensitive topic, potential policy violation, or ambiguous instruction), it should refrain rather than act.
  - It should treat any future user instructions as authoritative only if they clearly match its governance scope; otherwise it should not act.

3. Daily schedule configuration
- A recurring scheduled job has been created for the sub-agent:
  - Job name: "Daily X-control run"
  - Job ID: job_1772763318602_rim9x
  - Schedule: Every day at 09:00 (09:00) in the America/New_York timezone
- Behavior of the daily run:
  - At the scheduled time, the sub-agent is invoked to perform its X-related routine (e.g., checking for tasks, preparing or posting content, and performing any agreed governance checks).
  - The schedule is recurring and will continue to trigger daily unless explicitly modified or disabled.

4. Recording and traceability
- The schedule configuration and its purpose have been recorded in the workspace memory under a "Scheduling" section, noting:
  - The existence of the daily X-control job
  - Its role in managing @Small_Claw_’s X governance at the specified time and timezone

If you’d like, the next step can be to refine the sub-agent’s exact daily checklist (e.g., what it should verify, what kinds of posts to prepare, and any reporting format after each run).

that was beautiful - I dont necessarily need it doing anything as far as checking anything. I literally just need it daily posting for the SmallClaw X account - it should be posting things to Hype up SmallClaw and what not
